page: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-3200s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The hardware configuration is bundled with 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The underlying AMD EPYC Genoa processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX-512, AVX-512 VNNI, and AVX-512 BF16) for vector and machine learning workloads, alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. However, a key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation model, which may introduce resource contention compared to dedicated-core instances.

In synthetic benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong processing capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 3144.58 places it in the top tier (top 10%), while the multi-core score of 100562.57 is rated as strong (top 25%). This high resource density of 256.0 GB RAM and 3200 GB NVMe SSD storage combined with shared CPU allocation offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads that do not require sustained, non-blocking CPU performance.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database hosting, caching layers, and data processing pipelines that benefit from high memory capacity and fast local NVMe storage, provided they can tolerate shared CPU scheduling.
description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores on an AMD EPYC Genoa x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB per core, and includes 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While the CPU allocation is shared, the instance supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions such as AVX-512 and AES. In benchmark testing, the server achieved a top-tier single-core stress-ng score of 3144.58 and a strong multi-core score of 100562.57. This high memory and storage density offers a cost-efficient profile for resource-heavy workloads. It is suited for large databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing applications that can operate within a shared CPU environment.

tagline: A high-density memory and NVMe storage instance featuring AMD EPYC Genoa processors for data-intensive and memory-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs with 256.0 GB of system memory
3200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core stress-ng benchmark performance
Shared CPU allocation model offering high resource density
Suited for large databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications
